text: The surgeon observes a local perforation and phlegmon in the retrocecal appendix, which is embedded within the cecal fat. The surgeon attempts to elevate the appendix from the sidewall to better identify the anatomy, including the anti-mesenteric fat on the terminal ileum and the base of the appendix.

The surgeon observes evidence of a local perforation and a phlegmon in the retrocecal appendix, which is embedded within the cecal fat. The surgeon attempts to elevate the appendix from the sidewall, examining the anti-mesenteric fat on the terminal ileum and identifying the base of the appendix near the perforation site.
The surgeon attempts to create a window at the base of the appendix but decides to remove the anti-mesenteric fat from the terminal ileum to improve visibility of the appendix base.

The surgeon attempts to create a window at the base of the appendix but struggles to find a suitable location. The decision is made to remove the anti-mesenteric fat from the terminal ileum to improve visibility of the appendix base.

The surgeon attempts to visualize the body and tip of the appendix, cleaning the surgical field. They proceed to score the lateral aspect of the anticipated mesoappendix, carefully avoiding nearby structures like the cecum.

The surgeon attempts to visualize the body and tip of the appendix but is unable to do so. They proceed to clean the surgical field and then carefully score the lateral aspect of the anticipated mesoappendix, ensuring not to damage nearby structures such as the cecum.

The camera is positioned in the left lower quadrant, providing a view of the appendix embedded within cecal fat in a retroperitoneal location. The surgeon mobilizes the right colon to elevate the appendix, carefully opening the surrounding tissue while avoiding the cecum. The body of the appendix is exposed as the surgeon anticipates its trajectory towards the tip.

The camera is positioned in the left lower quadrant, revealing the appendix embedded within cecal fat in a retroperitoneal location. The surgeon mobilizes the right colon to elevate the appendix, carefully opening the surrounding tissue while avoiding the cecum. The body of the appendix becomes visible as the surgeon anticipates its trajectory.

The surgeon uses an inferior view to detach the lateral attachments of the cecal fat, revealing the appendix cocooned within the cecal wall. The appendix is peeled off the cecum, allowing better visualization of the mesoappendix. The surgeon prepares to use a ligature for efficient dissection.

The surgeon uses an inferior view to detach the lateral attachments of cecal fat, revealing the appendix cocooned within the cecal wall. The appendix is lifted off the cecum, allowing better visualization of the mesoappendix. The surgeon prepares to proceed with dissection using a ligature device.

The surgeon attempts to visualize the mesoappendix by repositioning the camera inferiorly. Using surgical instruments, they transect the anti-mesenteric fat of the terminal ileum to access the base of the appendix and its junction with the cecum. The tip of the appendix is freed, and the surgeon clears the base to prepare for stapling.

The surgeon attempts to visualize the mesoappendix by repositioning the view inferiorly. They transect the anti-mesenteric fat of the terminal ileum to access the base of the appendix and the junction with the cecum. With the tip of the appendix now free, the surgeon clears the base to prepare for stapling.

The surgeon removes the appendix through the umbilical port and irrigates the surgical area. The dissection site is checked for any inadvertent colotomies, confirming the colon appears intact. The surgical field is covered with omentum, and the area looks clean post-procedure.

The surgeon removes the appendix through the umbilical port and irrigates the area, ensuring the surgical field is clean. The dissection site is checked for any inadvertent colotomies, confirming the colon is intact. The omentum is used to cover the area.
